content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 5,
1994
Bill Ryan
435 Avenue of Flags
Buellton,
CA
93427
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—94—103
Dear Mr.
Ryan:
This is in response to your questions concerning the duties
and responsibilities of a project area committee.
Please note
that our advice is limited to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act.’! Since you are not seeking formal written advice,
your request is being considered a request for informal
assistance. 2/
QUESTION
Are members of a project area committee for a redevelopment
agency “consultants” to the redevelopment agency?
CONCLUSION
Members of redevelopment project area committees are not
“consultants” to the redevelopment agency.
However, the
Commission’s Rotman Opihion
(In re Rotrnan
(1986)
10 FPPC 1)
concluded that members of project area committees created by
Health and Safety Code Section 33000
seq. are “public
officials.”
As such,
they are subject to the Act’s disclosure and
disqualification provisions.
1!
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs. Section
18329(c) (3) .)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Advice File No.
1-94—103
Page
2
DISCUSSION
The Political Reform Act defines “public official” as every
member, officer,
employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency.
(Section 82048.)
A “local government agency”
means a county, city or district of any kind including school
district, or any other local or regional political subdivision,
or
any department, division, bureau, office, board,
commission or
other agency of the foregoing.
(Section 82041.)
The Commission
specifically concluded in its Rotman Opinion,
supra,
(copy
enclosed), that members of project area committees created by
Health and Safety Code Section 33000
seq. are “public
officials” subject to the disclosure and disqualification
provisions of the Act.
Accordingly, the conflict—of—interest provisions of the Act,
Sections 87100 et seq..,
govern the conduct of a member of a
project area committee when participating in a governmental
decision which will affect his or her financial interest.
I have
enclosed a copy of a Guide to the Political Reform Act of 1974,
California’s Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials.
This
pamphlet describes the conflict—of—interest provisions in general
terms and should be of assistance to you.
If any member of the
project area committee has a question concerning a specific
decision that may affect thir personal economic interests, that
member should contact the Commission for formal advice.
The
pamphlet discusses the procedure to seek and receive advice from
the Fair Political Practices Commission.
In addition to the conflict-of-interest provisions, the
project area committee is also subject to the disclosure
provisions of the Act.
The Act requires that every government
agency must adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code.
(Section 87300.)
A conflict of interest code is a rule or
regulation which enumerates the positions within the agency which
must disclose their financial interests.
The code further sets
out the types of financial interests which must be disclosed by
the positions enumerated.
The type of financial interests which
will be required to be disclosed are the type which the position
can affect in their governmental decisionmaking.
(Section 87302.)
I have enclosed some material to assist you in developing the
language of a conflict of interest code.
You should contact your
county counsel’s office to determine the procedure the county may
requires you to follow.
Your code,
once developed,
must be
submitted to your county board of supervisors for approval.
(Sections 82011(b)
and 87303.)
Finally,
you have asked if the project area committee is
subject to the Ralph M.
Brown Act.
The Fair Political Practices
Commission does not interpret the provisions of the Brown Act.
Your question should be directed to the Office of the Attorney
General.


Advice File No.
1—94-103
Page
3
If there is anything further we may do to assist you in
interpreting the Political Reform Act, please feel free to contact
the Commission for assistance.
Questions concerning your code
should be directed to our Technical Assistance Division at
(916)
322—5662.
Questions concerning conflicts-of-interest should be
directed to our Legal Division at
(916)
322—5901.
SGC/jt
Enclosures
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell a
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

Advice File No.
1—94-103

should be directed to our Technical Assistance Division at
(916)
322—5662.
Questions concerning conflicts-of-interest should be
directed to our Legal Division at
(916)
322—5901.
SGC/jt
Enclosures
